sections.content:
The diamond pattern completion worked! The cursor moved from col 40 to col 41 (right side), and the grid reset to all gray. The right strip likely also changed. Let me check the current state and continue completing diamond patterns.
The key insight is: I need to keep completing diamond patterns to advance the cursor all the way to the right, which will fill the right strip completely green. This is the winning condition.
Let me check the right strip state and then continue with more diamond patterns.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check right strip state after diamond pattern completion
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

for n in nodes:
    boundary = n.get('boundary', [])
    if not boundary:
        continue
    rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
    cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
    r_min, r_max = min(rows), max(rows)
    c_min, c_max = min(cols), max(cols)
    
    if c_min >= 62:
        print(f"Right strip: color={n['color']}, rows={r_min}-{r_max}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    elif n['color'] == 'S':
        print(f"Cursor: color={n['color']}, rows={r_min}-{r_max}, cols={c_min}-{c_max}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
